mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.4pp, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that drives corporate revenue expectations higher, producing positive forward equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 52%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation indicates healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, which supports corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO readings during low inflation periods yield negative 20-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the regime-specific alpha decays to zero within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage bills that boost consumer demand, leading to positive equity market performance over the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns with a win rate exceeding 50%, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.05 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5%, monetary policy shifts from accommodative to restrictive, raising the cost of capital and generating negative forward equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5%, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative over a 36-month evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates corporate profit concentration outside consumer sectors, which suppresses broad market returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance, or the interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample testing over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a high unemployment regime signals a deepening economic contraction that erodes corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO readings during high unemployment periods produce positive 5-day returns in over 65% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when tested against a buy-and-hold benchmark.
